differences: The interface uses FillWork's dark document-oriented shell instead of Slack's brighter workspace chrome.
Message bubbles remain restrained to preserve B2B information density.
Field photo analysis and voice transcription receive more prominence than in general-purpose chat products.
suggestions: Connect Socket.io message delivery and channel presence in the chat implementation phase.
Add keyboard navigation, thread replies, and real attachment previews after authentication is available.
Validate recording and location permission flows on the PWA mobile surface before release.
reasoning: The layout clearly reads as a mature collaboration messenger while remaining part of the existing FillWork product family. Channel navigation is fast to scan, the conversation preserves context without visual noise, and the location, field-photo, emergency, and voice-to-text patterns establish a specific field-work identity rather than a generic Slack clone.
